title: Motor Mechanic
description: As a senior member of the workshop, you will lead the charge on technical execution: * **Advanced Diagnostics:** Utilize modern diagnostic tools to identify and troubleshoot complex mechanical and electrical faults. * **Full\-Spectrum Repairs:** Execute everything from engine rebuilds and clutch replacements to timing belts and brake systems. * **Quality Assurance:** Conduct thorough inspections and road tests to ensure all work meets the highest safety and performance standards. * **Efficiency \& Excellence:** Manage a busy workload with precision, ensuring vehicles are returned to customers on time and in peak condition. * **Mentorship:** Provide technical guidance to junior staff or apprentices when required to maintain workshop standards. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Experience: * Mechanic : 4 years (required) Work Location: In person

title: Mulesoft Success Architect
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Customer Success Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. We’re Salesforce, the Customer Company, inspiring the future of business with AI\+ Data \+CRM. Leading with our core values, we help companies across every industry blaze new trails and connect with customers in a whole new way. And, we empower you to be a Trailblazer, too — driving your performance and career growth, charting new paths, and improving the state of the world. If you believe in business as the greatest platform for change and in companies doing well and doing good – you’ve come to the right place. At Salesforce, we are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where individuals from all backgrounds are welcomed and valued. We believe that the unique perspectives and skills of diverse candidates greatly contribute to the success of our teams. As a MuleSoft Success Architect, you will play a crucial role in driving successful outcomes for our strategic customers by leveraging your technical expertise in integration and API best practices. **Responsibilities** * Become a MuleSoft subject\-matter expert for the broader Success Architect organization, including related enterprise architecture concepts, governance and cross\-cloud solutions. * Engage with Signature Success customers to evaluate and recommend optimization strategies for technical architecture, dev/ops, performance, and solution design specific to MuleSoft. * Develop delivery and discovery assets aligned with the SA engagement catalog, tailored to the unique needs and opportunities of MuleSoft customers. * Build strong relationships with both internal and external business partners, contributing to broader goals and growth. **Impact Of The Role** As a MuleSoft Success Architect, you will have a significant impact on our customers' success and the growth of our organization. Your technical expertise and business acumen will directly influence the technical architecture, performance optimization, and solution design strategies for our Signature Success customers, driving success and business outcomes with MuleSoft's powerful analytics capabilities. By driving customer satisfaction and delivering exceptional value, you will contribute to the overall growth and reputation of Salesforce as a leader in the industry. **Collaboration And Teamwork** Collaboration is at the core of our success, and as a MuleSoft Success Architect, you will have the opportunity to work closely with diverse teams of professionals. You will collaborate with customers, colleagues, and partners to evaluate technical architecture, optimize performance, and design effective solutions. By fostering strong relationships and working collaboratively, you will contribute to the collective success of our teams and the achievement of our goals. **Basic Requirements** * A broad perspective on API and integration patterns, use cases, technologies, and trends. * Significant experience as a prior MuleSoft practitioner, with hands\-on experience in large scaled integration deployments. * A recognized and certified MuleSoft expert and broad technologist, with a deep understanding of the Anypoint Platform including its capabilities, limitations, design, and roadmap. * Expert domain knowledge in Performance, Scaling, DevOps and Containerization including working knowledge of Kubernetes. * Ability to evaluate architecture and infrastructure to uncover technical needs. Based on these discoveries, make recommendations for improvement with different lines of business while making use of the MuleSoft platform and supporting applications. * Demonstrated ability to analyze, design, and optimize business processes using technology and integration, with a focus on guiding customers through optimization and migration. * Ability to serve as a Trusted Advisor, driving conversations with Enterprise Architects, CTOs/CIOs, and Business Stakeholders armed with best practices for enterprise architecture functions such as Security, Performance, Development Process, and Application Governance. * Ability to advise and mentor diverse teams using influence and adaptive communication strategies. * Comfortable multitasking across parallel work streams, juggling different roles and priorities in each. * A strong willingness and comfort in diving into new situations, learning about new technologies quickly, and debating different approaches and points of view. * Excellent written, presentation and overall communication skills. * Proactive and self\-starting attitude with the ability to manage tasks independently. **Preferred Requirements** * Familiarity with micro\-services, cloud architecture and considerations for on\-prem vs Hybrid vs Cloud platforms. * Experience supporting/troubleshooting scaling and performance issues related to integration across distributed systems is advantageous. * Demonstrated thought leadership through publications, public speaking and others. * Past experiences leading enterprise wide initiatives, giving you an understanding of the challenges accomplishing widespread organizational change and the hindsight to address them. * Experience in a customer\-facing, sales\-aligned role such as a professional services consultant/architect, solutions architect or in a customer success role is a plus. Join our dynamic team and contribute to our inclusive and diverse work environment. We embrace diversity and are committed to creating equal opportunities for all applicants. At Salesforce, we value the unique perspectives and skills that individuals from diverse backgrounds bring to the table. If you are passionate about data analytics, technical leadership, driving customer success, and making a positive impact through collaboration, we invite you to apply for the MuleSoft Success Architect position. Help us shape the future of our Success Architect offering for MuleSoft while making a significant impact on our diverse and inclusive workplace. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Omnipay Client Services Level 2 Analyst
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Omnipay Client Services Level 2 Analyst**What does a successful Client Services Level 2 Analyst do?** As a Client Services Level 2 Support team member, you provide front line technical support for our clients across the globe. You define the client experience by working with internal teams who support clients and resolving queries within agreed service levels. You bring a strong passion for analysis with a keen focus on quality and detail, and are genuinely interested in understanding the whole technology stack. You communicate your analysis with great clarity and are able to discuss technical situations at a detailed level as a trusted partner in possibility. **What you will do:** * Resolve technical client queries received through multiple channels via in\-depth analysis or in conjunction with internal teams * Take ownership for query analysis, investigation and resolution in line with agreed service levels * Analyse statistical and production data to identify patterns and suggest improvements to the Team Leader and Manager for Fiserv and client business management * Develop new SQL processes to enhance data extract requirements Manage escalated queries and client\-critical issues that may hinder achievement of agreed service levels * Ensure the business is kept informed of application issues in a timely and clear manner * Interact with other team members and management to improve efficiency and effectiveness of the unit * Work continuously with the Team Manager and independently to design and create data reports and reporting tools that enhance day\-to\-day operations * Provide on\-call, bank\-holiday and weekend support on a rotational basis **What you will need to have:** * Practical experience creating or maintaining SQL\-based data extracts and reporting solutions * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Self\-motivation with the ability to work on own initiative or as part of a team * Working knowledge of Excel and VBA for data analysis and reporting * Strong quality focus with high attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to work under pressure to tight deadlines with a clear commitment to service delivery **What would be great to have:** * Experience collaborating with Level 1 Support, Service Managers and Level 3 Production Support teams * Proven capability in managing escalated and client\-critical issues in a production support environment * Demonstrated ability to interpret statistical and production data to drive process improvements * Confidence in communicating with employees and stakeholders at all levels within the company Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please: * Apply using your legal name * Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). **Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:** Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. **Note to agencies:** Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. **Warning about fake job posts:** Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon's global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon's control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon's risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills** **and Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues \#LI\-SB1 **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It's at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it's our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. That's the real meaning of studio\-to\-street: taking the consciousness from practice on the mat and putting it into practice in life. **Objective** The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving to profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. **This is a 6\-month, fixed\-term contract.** **Key Job Responsibilities** * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead. * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; including but not limited to visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence * Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business\-driving strategies * Resolve client needs quickly \& effectively ensuring customer satisfaction * Identify product concerns and communicate inventory needs to support the business goals * Support other areas of the business, including Sales \& Service and Cashier, as needed **Operations Associate Qualifications** * Preferred prior work experience in a client\-centric, sales \& operational environment * Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences * High energy, upbeat and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work * Self\-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually, and as a team * Fosters genuine connection through empathy, compassion, integrity and building trusting relationships * Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles * Proof of right to live and work in the UK **Operations Associate Schedule** Part\-Time associates are expected to work up to 29 hours per week; Full\-Time associates are expected to work 32\-40 hours per week with 100% of their work week in operations and floor support. All employees' availability must suit the needs of the business, which are subject to change. The General Manager determines availability needs and creates a schedule including morning, evening, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change. All Employees are expected to be able to work from the week of and including "Black Friday" until the end of the holiday season, first week of January. **Diversity and Inclusion statement** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, ALO does not discriminate against applicants or employees because of race, colour, creed, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, age, citizenship, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression or because of any other status or condition protected by statutory law. We are committed to an active Inclusion, Diversity and Equal Opportunities Policy, which starts with our recruitment and selection process, and we are happy to talk flexible working. **Interview Process adjustments** If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application or by emailing europehr@aloyoga.com. We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. \#LI\-JJ1 \#LI\-2 \#li\-onsite For CA residents, Job Applicant Privacy Policy HERE.

title: People & Culture Coordinator (Graduate)
description: DOCOsoft is seeking a motivated and curious People \& Culture Coordinator (Graduate) to join our team. Reporting to the Head of People and Culture, this role will support the delivery of core People \& Culture activities across the employee lifecycle, while gaining exposure to areas such as recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and organisational development. This is a development role designed to build strong foundational HR capability, working as part of the People \& Culture team across People Operations, Talent Acquisition, and Business Partnering activities. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support day\-to\-day People \& Culture activities across the employee lifecycle, including onboarding, offboarding, and maintaining accurate employee records within HR systems (e.g. BambooHR). * Coordinate recruitment processes, including interview scheduling, candidate communication, and supporting hiring managers through the process. * Assist in delivering a smooth onboarding experience and contribute to employee communications and engagement activities. * Contribute to People \& Culture initiatives such as engagement surveys, wellbeing activities, and culture programmes. * Support the preparation of reports, presentations, and people data to inform leadership decision\-making. * Assist with the coordination of learning and development activities and internal programmes. * Provide administrative support on employee relations matters, including documentation and note\-taking, while building understanding of policies and processes. * Support performance management processes, including goal tracking and review cycle administration. * Ensure accuracy, confidentiality, and professionalism when handling employee information. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of People \& Culture processes and documentation as the organisation evolves. **Skills and Qualifications:** * Recently completed or working towards a degree in Human Resources, Business, or a related field. * Strong organisational skills with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks. * Good communication skills and confidence interacting with colleagues across the organisation. * A proactive approach with a willingness to learn and take ownership of work. * Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and professionalism. * Familiarity with Microsoft Office tools; experience with HR systems is an advantage. **Who we are:** DOCOsoft is a leading software and services provider to Lloyd’s of London and the broader London insurance market. Since our foundation, we have grown to become one of the leading insurance software specialists in the London Insurance Market. We are a growing team of over 90 colleagues based in Dublin, London, Tokyo, Portugal, Spain, India and Poland. DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an and expression of their own ideas. **Here’s what we have to offer:** DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an expression of their own ideas. We offer our people: * The opportunity to impact our growing business\- everyone’s contribution matters! * A team environment that is focused on the creation and delivery of great products for our clients. * Exciting challenges to grow and enhance their skills. * A competitive pay and benefits package (private pension, bonus scheme, private health, life assurance, flexible working, 25 days annual leave). **Equal Opportunity Employer** DOCOsoft is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team that represents a variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, and do not discriminate on the grounds of race, religion, gender, marital or family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, membership of the travelling community or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Should you require reasonable accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Plumber
description: With over 25 years of experience in delivering specialist maintenance and successfully completing thousands of complex projects, our organization has the specific expertise required to operate safely and efficiently in restricted and live environments. Our continued successes highlight the quality of work carried out by our team of highly skilled technicians, and the professional service we provide. We take great pride in exceeding our clients' expectations and maintaining a high standard of excellence. We are currently seeking a skilled **Plumber** to join our growing team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in plumbing maintenance and repair, with experience in a variety of settings, including restricted and live environments. **Key Responsibilities:** * Perform assigned tasks in accordance with professional standards. * Ensure adherence to high\-quality work standards throughout all projects. * Follow through on designated tasks to completion. * Responsively address client inquiries and concerns. * Proactively resolve any issues that may arise during projects. * Provide guidance and support to junior staff as needed. * Delegate tasks effectively to apprentices and subcontractors. * Uphold a safe working environment for all team members. * Maintain compliance with rigorous Health and Safety protocols. * Collaborate closely with senior site team members to achieve project objectives. **Requirements:** * Proven experience in a plumbing role, ideally with several years of practical experience. * Fully qualified through FAS/SOLAS (or international equivalent). * Domestic and commercial experience * Strong communication skills and a collaborative approach. * Valid driving license (mandatory). **What We Offer:** * Competitive salary package. * A supportive work environment that fosters professional growth and development. * Opportunities to work alongside a dedicated team of skilled professionals. * Full\-time, permanent position with stability and career advancement prospects. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Plumbing: 5 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * Safe Pass (preferred) * Driving Licence (preferred) * OFTEC registration (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Power Platform Solutions Engineer

You’ll work closely with solution architects, business analysts, and stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable, secure applications. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and build applications using Power Apps (Canvas \& Model\-Driven) * Develop workflows and automations with Power Automate * Create dashboards and reporting solutions with Power BI * Work with Dynamics 365 and Dataverse to support data\-driven solutions * Integrate systems using APIs, SharePoint, and other data sources * Contribute to solution design, data modelling, and architecture decisions * Participate in client workshops and help shape technical solutions * Support deployments using Azure DevOps (CI/CD, ALM practices) **Requirements** * 3\+ years’ experience working with Microsoft Power Platform * Strong experience with Power Apps (Canvas and/or Model\-Driven) * Hands\-on experience with Power Automate * Experience with Dynamics 365 and/or Dataverse * Solid understanding of Power BI * Experience with integrations (APIs, SharePoint, SQL, etc.) * Exposure to JavaScript and/or Power Fx * Familiarity with Azure DevOps / CI\-CD is beneficial * Experience working in Agile environments **Soft Skills** * Comfortable working directly with clients and stakeholders * Experience gathering requirements and running workshops * Strong problem\-solving and troubleshooting skills * Ability to work across multiple projects and industries **What’s On Offer** * Hybrid working model (2 days onsite, 3 remote) * Exposure to modern Microsoft technologies and projects * Opportunity to gain certifications and ongoing professional development * Work across a variety of industries and client environments Apply Now

title: Process Mechanical Engineer
description: **Adjutant Solutions Group (ASG)** ASG specializes in the development, qualification, and commercialization of drug delivery systems for biomedical products. We deliver integrated business and science solutions to foster healthier, safer, and more prosperous communities. Guided by our core values—service, client success, curiosity, respect, integrity, and accountability—we value diversity and prioritize collaboration and growth within our inclusive, team\-oriented culture. Check out our GOASG website. ASG Europe Limited is a subsidiary of ASG LLC. This is a unique opportunity to help shape ASG and its expansion into Ireland and Europe. **Process Mechanical Engineer** We are seeking a proficient Process Mechanical Engineer (PME, Level 2\) at Adjutant Solutions Group (ASG). The PME will have a hybrid role to design, develop, and implement automation solutions for commercial device assembly and packaging. You will lead complex projects with minimal supervision, provide technical expertise, and collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver industry\-compliant solutions. Responsibilities include technical leadership in Device Assembly, Packaging, project leadership, risk management, and verification activities, while mentoring junior team members and engaging stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities:** * Technical Expertise: Stay updated on process mechanical engineering trends, providing expertise to acquirers, suppliers, and delivery teams. Lead ASGs efforts in Medical Device Assembly and Packaging * Project Leadership: Define project scope, align priorities, and manage execution to meet objectives for scope, time, and budget * Design \& Engineering: Analyze stakeholder needs, develop solution architectures, understand CAD drawings, ensuring compliance with industry standards * Delivery \& Implementation: Design, validate, and integrate automated assembly and packaging solutions, overseeing procurement and fabrication * Verification: Execute and document verification activities, including Design of Experiments (DOE), Engineering Studies, FAT, SAT and compliance with safety and quality standards * Advocacy: Represent client interests with suppliers and advise on product design impacts for manufacturing and assembly **Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in mechanical, or biomedical engineering or related field * 2\-5 years of relevant experience **Knowledge, Skills and Abilities** : * Process mechanical engineering principles (material flows, safety/quality by design, pneumatics, intelligent actuators/sensors) * Automated assembly, sequential operations design, power distribution, component fabrication, machining, and tolerance verification * Critical thinking, analytical reasoning (applied math, decision dependencies, technical critical path) * Systems thinking (interdependency, systems analysis/engineering) * Communication (clear, concise, objective oral/written articulation) * Project management, time management, and organizational skills * Problem\-solving (cause\-and\-effect identification, collaborative trade space alternatives) * Overseeing small\- to medium\-scale projects and leading technical projects * Independent process engineering and manufacturing process design refinement * Collaboration on material selection, design feasibility, and aesthetics with cross\-functional teams * Researching new materials, manufacturing technologies, and finish options * Informing prototype/production cost estimations (tooling, assembly, finishing, piece part) * Experience using metrology software platforms, particularly CT * Strong understanding of GD\&T **Core Values:** * Accountability: Owning the consequences of your decisions and actions * Integrity: Complete harmony in what you think, say, and do concerning the ASG Way and our Code of Conduct * Respect: Treating people how you want to be treated, whether or not you agree with them * Curiosity: An enduring desire to learn and grow * Client Service: Results that address client needs based on client decisions * Service: Giving priority to enriching the lives of others **Benefits** * 22 days annual leave, with incremental increases based on years of service * Pension scheme with company match * Private healthcare scheme, fully covered by the company * Company social events to foster a collaborative and engaging work environment Powered by JazzHR 9F0TrDgcrZ

title: Product Analyst
description: **Job Title:** **Product Analyst** **Department: Product** **Reporting to:** **Director of Product** **Location: Hybrid / HQ / Remote** **Role Purpose** **Role Purpose and Key Responsibilities:** **We are looking for a Product Analyst to turn product data into clear insights and opportunities, helping our teams build better products and drive measurable commercial impact** **The Right Person Will Be** * Curious and proactive — finds opportunities and spots trends rather than just answers questions * Comfortable challenging PM thinking with data * Strong communicator — can simplify complex analysis and deliver to the Product team along with stakeholders * Outcome\-focused — cares about impact, not just reporting * Data driven to lead product decisions across Sports, Gaming and Core journeys * Provide clear visibility on product performance, customer behaviour and opportunity areas * Enable Product Managers to move from opinion\-led → evidence\-led prioritisation **Key Responsibilities:** **Product Performance \& Insight** * Own product performance tracking across key journeys (e.g. acquisition, onboarding, early player lifecycle funnel analysis, betting, retention and payments) * Analyse feature adoption, engagement and commercial impact post\-release * Identify friction points, drop\-offs and optimisation opportunities * Be curious, look for hidden trends and opportunities that the PMs should be aware of and capitalise on * Work with the research team to build out customer insight with real data and validation **Commercial \& Customer Analysis** * Build and maintain funnel analysis (e.g. registration → deposit → bet → repeat) * Segment users (new vs returning, high value, behavioural cohorts) * Quantify revenue or relevant KPI impact of product changes **Experimentation \& Optimisation** * Support A/B testing and experimentation design * Measure impact of tests and feature releases * Partner with PMs to prioritise high\-impact opportunities **Data Visualisation \& Reporting** * Build dashboards for PMs and leadership (self\-serve where possible) * Create weekly/monthly insight packs per domain * Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations **Product Team Partnership** * Work directly with PMs, Designers and Tech Leads * Shape hypotheses and define success metrics pre\-build * Embed into product squads where needed **Key Outputs (What Success Looks Like)** * Clear visibility of product performance by domain * Regular identification of measurable optimisation opportunities * Evidence\-based prioritisation across roadmaps * Increased feature adoption, conversion and retention * Stronger post\-release accountability **Specialist Skills and Experience Required** * Strong SQL (or equivalent) and data manipulation skills * Experience with product analytics tools * Experience building dashboards (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Power BI) * Strong commercial mindset — understands revenue drivers, not just metrics * Ability to translate data into clear product recommendations * Experience working closely with Product teams * A domain expert would be preferred with experience in the Betting and Gaming industry and an excellent understanding of its customers * Comfortable operating in a hands\-on delivery role, managing both strategy and execution * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills * Experience working within Agile development environments

title: Production Operator
description: **Job Title:** **Manufacturing Biotech Associate – Level 02** **Purpose of the Role:** This role is based at a state\-of\-the\-art biologics manufacturing facility designed to support clinical supply, product registration, and commercial manufacturing of biologic drug substances. The site operates in a multi\-product environment, requiring flexibility to adapt to evolving pipeline demands. The Manufacturing Biotech Associate is responsible for supporting manufacturing operations involved in the production of biologic drug substances on a designated shift. The role includes executing automated processes using paperless systems across upstream and downstream activities such as inoculation, cell culture, filtration, and purification. The position also involves the setup and operation of single\-use technologies, ensuring all activities are carried out in compliance with GMP and EHS standards. A strong focus is placed on continuous improvement, operational excellence, and delivering high\-quality products to patients through right\-first\-time execution. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support end\-to\-end manufacturing operations for biologics production. * Operate equipment in line with electronic batch records (EBR), SOPs, and sampling plans. * Ensure strict adherence to GMP, safety, and compliance standards. * Perform upstream and downstream processing activities including cell culture, filtration, and purification. * Set up and operate single\-use systems between manufacturing runs. * Complete all manufacturing documentation accurately in line with GMP requirements. * Support delivery of key performance indicators (KPIs) such as safety, quality, and production targets. * Apply Lean Six Sigma principles to drive continuous improvement initiatives. * Participate in troubleshooting and escalate issues as required. * Contribute to SOP and work instruction development and updates. * Engage in shift handovers, team meetings, and cross\-functional collaboration. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of manufacturing processes through ongoing training. * Support site safety and environmental initiatives. **Required Education, Experience \& Skills:** * Level 6 qualification in Science, Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). * Minimum 2 years of experience in a GMP manufacturing environment. * Understanding of contamination control and batch release processes. * Knowledge of upstream and downstream operations for biologics manufacturing. * Experience working in a team\-based, fast\-paced manufacturing environment. * Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow procedures accurately. **Preferred Skills \& Experience:** * Experience with single\-use technologies in biopharmaceutical manufacturing. * Familiarity with automation systems such as DeltaV and MES (e.g., PAS\-X). * Experience with SAP or similar systems. * Exposure to Lean manufacturing or continuous improvement methodologies. **Additional Information:** * Site\-based role * Requires flexibility to work a 24/7 shift pattern

title: QA/RA Assistant

The role also involves participation in internal and external audits and inspections, supporting preparation activities and follow\-up actions as required. In addition, the QARA Assistant supports the maintenance of Osteotec’s medical device technical files and batch records, and contributes to ensuring compliance across storage, handling and distribution processes. The position further supports risk management and continuous improvement activities, contributing to the overall effectiveness and compliance of the QMS. **Date: 16 April 2026** **Location:** Dublin office **Reports to:** Head of Quality Assurance and Regulatory Affairs (QARA) **Hours:** 37\.5 per week **Salary:** €38,000 to €40,000 (subject to experience) plus competitive benefits **Responsibilities:** **Quality Management System \& Document Control** * Support maintenance and improvement of the QMS in line with ISO 13485, EU MDR, UK MDR and other applicable requirements * Assist with document control processes (distribution, version control, archiving) ensuring documentation is accurate, controlled and up to date * Support change control activities by coordinating documentation updates and tracking changes to ensure appropriate assessment of quality and regulatory impact **Nonconformances, CAPA \& Complaints** * Track and log nonconformities, deviations, CAPAs and complaints within QMS systems, ensuring data accuracy and timely updates * Support the coordination of investigations, containment actions and follow\-up activities to ensure timely resolution of quality events * Assist with complaint handling processes, including logging, documentation and collation of supporting information **Audits, Metrics \& Training** * Support internal and external audit activities, including scheduling, preparation of documentation, recording findings and assisting with follow\-up actions * Assist in compiling QARA metrics and key performance indicators for reporting and management review * Maintain and track training records to support compliance with QMS requirements **Supplier \& Operational Quality Support** * Maintain supplier records and performance data, supporting qualification, evaluation and ongoing monitoring activities * Assist in the preparation, coordination and delivery of supplier audits and liaise with suppliers to obtain required documentation and information * Perform goods inward inspections for Osteotec and third\-party devices, ensuring compliance with defined acceptance criteria * Support compliance in storage, handling and distribution, including environmental monitoring and traceability **Technical Documentation \& Records** * Support the maintenance of medical device technical documentation, ensuring records are updated and controlled appropriately * Maintain batch records, calibration logs and other quality records to ensure completeness, accuracy and traceability **Regulatory \& Post\-Market Support** * Support post\-market surveillance activities by collecting, organising and maintaining relevant data * Assist in the preparation of documentation required for regulatory submissions, audits and inspections **General** * Contribute to risk management activities through data entry, record updates and tracking of risk controls * Support QARA\-related training and continuous improvement initiatives across the business **Qualifications \& Experience:** **Essential** * A degree in a relevant scientific discipline (e.g. life sciences, biomedical, engineering or similar) * global manufacturers. **Our Vision** To be the partner of choice for orthopaedic extremities through our unparalleled expertise, outstanding service and tailored solutions. **Our Values** **People title: QC Analyst - Quality Operations Laboratory
description: **Purpose** Join a supportive, growing team in the analysis of Biologics and Vaccines in a state\-of\-the\-art Quality Operations laboratory. Carlow will manufacture and test biggest selling biologic and the biggest selling vaccine product and is a strategic manufacturing site. The **QC Analyst / Senior Technician Quality Control** will report directly to the QC Manager and will help us in the expansion of this Laboratory and complete analytical method transfers and validation as well as commercial testing. The lab operates a 2 cycle and 4 cycle shift and roles are available in both. Occasional travel may be required for training purposes. **Responsibilities** **Bring energy, knowledge, innovation to carry out the following:** * Work as directed by the Quality Control Manager according to Company safety policies, cGMP and cGLP. Required to drive compliance with Global policies, procedures and guidelines and regulatory requirements and execute Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) in the performance of day\-to\-day activities and all applicable job functions * Participate in QC Tier 1 daily meetings and ensure effective communication of testing progress, deviations etc * Participate in writing/revising/ rolling out accurate operational procedures, training materials and maintenance procedures for various Quality systems; ensure all work is carried out in line with same * Operate as part of the QC team performing the allocated testing and laboratory\-based duties * Perform various analytical techniques including but not limited to HPLC, (HIC, CE, IEX, HP\-SEC), Capillary Electrophoresis and other compendial test methods in compliance with GMP requirements * Peer review testing documentation and ensuring data integrity compliance and QC Right First Time KPIs are achieved * Participate in the laboratory aspects of OOS investigations * Provide support with audit/inspection requirements to ensure department compliance/readiness. * Participate in internal and external audits and inspections, taking the role of auditee for assigned areas of responsibility. * Drive continuous improvement, perform root cause analysis on system failures and substandard equipment performance, using standard tools and methods, to resolve machine and system issues e.g., FMEA, Fishbone diagrams, 5 why's etc, * Work collaboratively to drive a safe and compliant culture on site. **Requirements** * Bachelors Degree or higher preferred; ideally in Analytical Chemistry/ Biochemistry or a closely related discipline * The ideal candidate will have 2\- 3 years' experience in a pharmaceutical laboratory, ideally with experience in HPLC and relevant systems and software. Core to the role is to perform testing of samples to support the release of our key drug products. * A good working knowledge of HPLC systems and software is desirable * A good knowledge of cGMP, GLP, Quality Management Systems **Desired Skills and Experience**

title: QC Microbiologist Analyst
description: **Location: Dublin College Park** **12 hour shifts 7am to 7pm \- 2 week rotation** **Wk 1: Mon, Tues, Sat , Sun** **Wk 2: Wed, Thurs, Fri** **Competitive Salary And Benefits** **Opening date: 21/04/2026** **Closing date: midnight 27/04/2026** **Introduction To Role** This QC Microbiologist Analyst role in Dublin focuses on microbiological testing and environmental monitoring to support clinical and commercial manufacturing. The position involves working in a cGMP QC laboratory, operating microbiology equipment, following written SOPs, recording data in real time, and maintaining strong attention to detail. Routine tasks are carried out with minimal supervision while contributing to reliable product release and sterility assurance for patients with serious and rare conditions. **Accountabilities** Carry out environmental and facilities monitoring sampling and testing, ensuring accurate, timely collection and analysis of data. Perform microbiological testing to support release and stability programs on site, using appropriate Micro techniques. Maintain high cGMP and GLP standards during all testing activities, meeting schedules and targets to satisfy regulatory and business requirements. Ensure a high standard of housekeeping and safety in the laboratory, promoting safe working practices at all times. Complete project work and implement initiatives that improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the laboratory function. Keep management informed of issues arising within the laboratory, escalating risks and proposing solutions. Train laboratory personnel in relevant microbiology methods, procedures and standards, contributing to the design, delivery and evaluation of training and re\-training programs. **Essential Skills/Experience** * Proven experience in cGMP Quality environment. * Experience in testing of sterile/non\-sterile pharmaceutical /biopharmaceutical products. * Sound knowledge of current regulatory requirements for Microbiology / Sterility Assurance in support of cGMP operations supporting clinical and commercial manufacturing. * Degree in Microbiology or related science. * The duties of this role are generally conducted in an lab environment. As is typical of a lab\-based role, employees must be able, with or without an accommodation to: lift/carry 15/30 pounds unassisted/assisted; work comfortably in a controlled environment with and around biological, infectious, and hazardous materials; gown/degown PPE; use a computer; engage in communications via phone, video, and electronic messaging; engage in problem solving and non\-linear thought, analysis, and dialogue; collaborate with others; maintain general availability during standard business hours. **Desirable Skills/Experience** * Experience/Knowledge of aseptic techniques At AstraZeneca, work connects directly to people living with rare and devastating diseases, turning complex science into meaningful impact every day. Colleagues collaborate across disciplines with the energy of a biotech and the reach of a global biopharma, exploring new frontiers in rare disease while staying grounded in integrity and patient stories that guide decisions. Learning never stands still here: tailored development, open dialogue and an inclusive culture encourage fresh thinking, experimentation and continuous improvement. Every contribution matters, from refining a lab method to shaping a new therapy area, as teams come together with determination to change lives now and build better options for tomorrow. Ready to explore rare opportunities and help shape what comes next in rare disease? Apply now! **Date Posted** 21\-Apr\-2026 **Closing Date** 27\-Apr\-2026 Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. title: Quality Technician
description: **Purpose** The Laboratory Technician will support GMP laboratory operations by ensuring that laboratory facilities, equipment, and consumables are maintained in a compliant, safe, and efficient manner. The role is critical in sustaining laboratory readiness through effective cleaning, inventory management, and adherence to health, safety, and environmental requirements. **Responsibilities** Laboratory Support \& Housekeeping Perform glass washing, drying, and appropriate storage of laboratory glassware in compliance with GMP requirements. Clean and sterilize laboratory equipment, utensils, and work surfaces to maintain a safe and compliant working environment. Carry out routine cleaning of the Central Testing Unit (CTU) in accordance with approved procedures and schedules. Maintain 5S standards for laboratory areas, including fridges and freezers, ensuring organization, cleanliness, and clear labelling at all times. **Inventory \& Materials Management** Monitor laboratory consumables and inventory levels on a routine basis. Restock laboratory supplies as required to ensure uninterrupted laboratory operations. Report inventory shortages, expired materials, or damaged items to senior laboratory staff in a timely manner. Ensure materials are stored according to defined conditions and GMP requirements. **Health, Safety \& Environmental Compliance** Strictly adhere to health and safety regulations, including the correct use of personal protective equipment (PPE). Dispose of hazardous and non\-hazardous laboratory waste in line with site procedures and regulatory requirements. Immediately report safety concerns, incidents, or near misses in accordance with MSD procedures. Support a strong safety\-first culture within the laboratory environment. **GMP \& Quality Compliance** Perform all assigned duties in compliance with cGMP, company policies, and site procedures. Accurately complete required documentation and records in line with data integrity principles. Support audit and inspection readiness by maintaining laboratory areas at required standards. **Communication \& Teamwork** Communicate effectively with laboratory colleagues and senior staff regarding laboratory status, inventory issues, and safety concerns. Work collaboratively as part of a GMP laboratory team to support daily operations and site objectives. **Key Skills \& Competencies** Attention to Detail: Ability to consistently maintain high standards of cleanliness, organization, and compliance. Safety Compliance: Strong awareness of laboratory safety, chemical handling, and waste disposal requirements. Organization: Ability to manage multiple tasks while maintaining orderly laboratory environments. Communication: Clear and effective communication when reporting issues, shortages, or safety concerns. Reliability: Demonstrates a dependable and responsible approach to routine laboratory duties. Teamwork: Works well with others to support laboratory and site goals. **Requirements** Previous experience working in a laboratory or GMP\-regulated environment is an advantage. Familiarity with laboratory housekeeping, inventory management, or regulated cleaning activities is beneficial. Training in health and safety or laboratory practices is desirable; full training will be provided.

title: Register your interest for our 2026 Digital Academy Programme
description: **Company Description** At Version 1, we believe in unlocking the potential of our Early Careers Associates. We have partnered with leading training providers to create bespoke academies that help our associates develop their IT skill sets and earn industry\-recognized certifications. The program's Early Careers framework enables you to build a strong network with peers across the business while advancing your career in technology. So far we have achieved some incredible accolades for our work: * Named 2023 Oracle EMEA Partner of the Year Award for Innovation * Named a Best Workplace™ UK in Tech by Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace UK for Women by Great Place to Work® * Named India’s \#1 Mid\-Size Workplace by Great Place to Work India® * Named Global Microsoft Partner of the Year for Modernising Applications 2023 * Named a Best Workplace™ Ireland in Tech by Great Place to Work® * Awarded Best Project of the Year at the UKOUG Awards 2023 * Accredited with Investors in Diversity Silver mark by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Named a Best Super Large Workplace™ UK By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ India for Building a Culture of Innovation by All By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ Ireland for Women by Great Place to Work® * Named No. 1 Best Large Workplace™ Ireland \- 2023 By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ UK for Wellbeing 2023 By Great Place to Work® * Named a Top 10 Best Place To Work UK By Glassdoor *What can you help us achieve in 2024?* **Job Description** * PLEASE NOTE THAT THIS IS FOR REGISTERING YOUR INTEREST IN FUTURE ACADEMIES ACROSS ALL VERSION 1 LOCATIONS. THIS IS NOT A LIVE VACANCY AND YOU WILL BE CONTACTED WHEN AN ACADEMY BECOMES AVAILABLE IN YOUR HOME LOCATION (PLEASE ENSURE THAT UPON APPLICATION, YOU HAVE INCLUDED WHERE YOU ARE BASED/ YOUR HOME LOCATION TO HELP US DIRECT YOUR APPLICATION).\*\* - "Our goal in Version 1 is to ensure that a career in technology is accessible to everyone. That's why our Academy is open to college/university graduates, career changers, returners to the workforce, and individuals with technical skills who want to experience a culture of recognition and reward. You will meet Version 1 Mentors and Senior Technologists to learn how to practically use new skills in a Consultancy role. This is a once\-in\-a\-lifetime opportunity that you simply cannot afford to miss out on. Take the first step towards a successful career by enrolling in our academy today. * First 12\-weeks on Academy: paid training contract * Upon successful completion of 12\-week Academy: final interview with opportunity for Permanent Contract as an Associate Consultant * Over the first 18 months as Associate Consultant you will be eligible for salary uplifts (based on your performance): + 6 months – 5% increase + 12 months – 5% increase + 18 months – 10% increase **Qualifications** **Key Requirements:** **Our Digital Skills Academies Would Be Great If You** * Have received a university degree classification of 2:2 in any ICT or STEM subject * Love getting stuck into problem\-solving and you don’t give up easily! * Are passionate about technology * Eligible to work in the UK/ Ireland * Eligibility and willingness to travel across offices in the EU and UK to meet with our stakeholders and customers. (Travel Costs covered by Version 1\) * An added bonus if you have previous IT work experience or you participated in a Tech Bootcamp/course **Additional Information** **Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer.** We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience.
